_readme: IMI Guard organization policy pack. This file is what makes 'unauthorized' mean something. With Enabled=false the tool still REPORTS hotspot/USB/tethering activity, but only as neutral observations, because it cannot know what your site permits. Set Enabled=true and fill in the rules below to have that same evidence classified as a POLICY VIOLATION. Fill this in BEFORE going to site.
_networks: Set InternetAllowed=false for an air-gapped/restricted PC. Set MobileHotspotAllowed=false where staff must not tether their phones. ApprovedNetworks is the list of SSIDs/network names staff are allowed to join - leave it empty to skip that check.
_usb: Set UsbStorageAllowed=false where removable media is banned outright. Otherwise list the approved devices by serial number (shown in the report for every USB device seen).
_software: Names matched case-insensitively against installed software.
_cloud: Data-movement policy. Set PersonalCloudStorageAllowed=false where staff must not sign personal cloud accounts (OneDrive Personal, Dropbox, Google Drive, MEGA...) into a work PC - a signed-in personal cloud client then becomes a POLICY VIOLATION. ApprovedCloudAccounts is the corporate accounts/domains that are fine: use a full address ('jane@corp.com') or a domain ('@corp.com'). Leave empty to treat every cloud account found as a review item.
_hours: Sign-ins outside these hours are reported for AUTHORIZATION REVIEW - never as an accusation. Leave Days empty to disable the check entirely.
